response:
Cover letter based on the following internship opportunity: This position is located in the Headquarters Client Support Service (HQCSS), Division of Administration, New York, Department of Operational Support (DOS). The intern will report to the Senior Human Resources Officer and Human Resources Officers within DOS. The Department of Operational Support provides operational advisory services to operating entities across the Secretariat, including other departments, offices away from headquarters, field missions, and regional commissions.

The internship duration is 6 months, depending on the needs of the office. Interns are expected to work four to five days per week, either remotely or in the assigned office, under the supervision of their supervisor (totaling 20 to 35 hours per week). Interns work as full team members, pursue projects designed to make a significant difference in HQCSS, and have opportunities to learn about the organization overall. Daily responsibilities will vary based on the individual's background, the assigned business unit, and the internship period.

HQCSS provides administrative support to numerous offices, including various Special Representative offices, the Ethics Office, the Global Compact Office, the Human Security Unit, the Office of Administration of Justice, the Office of Information and Communications Technology, the Office of the Ombudsman and Mediation Services, the Office of Special Envoy for Haiti, the Office of Victims' Rights Advocate, the Office of Special Coordinator on Improving the Response to Sexual Exploitation and Abuse, the Office for Partnerships, and liaison offices for the Office on Drugs and Crime and the Convention to Combat Desertification in New York.

Under the supervision and guidance of a Human Resources Officer, specific tasks may include:
*   Screening and reviewing document validity, completeness, and accuracy for on-boarding and extension requests from client offices in line with staff rules and regulations.
*   Liaising with client offices to ensure all required documentation is received.
*   Providing information and guidance to hiring managers and candidates regarding eligibility and visa requirements.
*   Assisting with the on-boarding of interns.
*   Providing statistics and running reports as required.
*   Uploading all administrative documents into the database using SharePoint.
*   Participating in record-keeping improvement projects, including evaluating the adequacy of existing records management and reference tools.
*   Assisting with other administrative duties as required, including evaluation, monitoring, and reporting on a wide range of HR-related issues.
*   Developing an inventory of documentation to be retained and recording them in office databases and shared drives.
*
